mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and expanding economy, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment generally correlates with economic growth
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth consistently leads to negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the correlation between PAYEMS growth and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in the unemployment rate, specifically an increase of 0.5 percentage points or more over three months, signals a deteriorating labor market and impending economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-quarter horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Recessions are typically preceded by rising unemployment
failure_condition: If a 0.5pp rise in UNRATE over 3 months is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or if the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) is below 0.05 during downturns.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is strong (above 0.5% month-over-month) while the federal funds rate remains low (below 1%), it indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending and low rates support equity markets
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative policy
failure_condition: If periods meeting the trigger conditions consistently result in negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS level is not statistically significant in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth (above 0.3% month-over-month), indicating robust manufacturing and supply-side expansion, the S&P 500 experiences positive forward returns over a one-quarter horizon, reflecting strong corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial growth is a key component of GDP and corporate earnings
regime_hint: economic expansion with strong industrial activity
failure_condition: If strong INDPRO growth periods are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 35% of instances, or if the correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX returns turns negative during expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden surge in housing starts (HOUST) indicates increasing construction activity and consumer confidence, which provides an immediate boost to economic sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity
regime_hint: any regime, but particularly effective during recoveries
failure_condition: If a 10% MoM rise in HOUST is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or if the signal's alpha is not positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation, indicated by CPIAUCSL rising above 0.4% month-over-month for two consecutive months, signals eroding purchasing power and potential for tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth > 0.4% MoM for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation can lead to higher interest rates and lower equity valuations
regime_hint: inflationary environment or monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: If sustained high CPIAUCSL growth is consistently followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal fails to produce a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is expanding (INDPRO growth > 0.2% MoM) but employment growth is decelerating (PAYEMS growth < 0.1% MoM), it suggests productivity gains without broad job creation, which can be interpreted as a 'jobless recovery' or efficiency gains, leading to mixed but generally positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-quarter horizon as profits rise without wage pressure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.2% MoM AND PAYEMS growth < 0.1% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Productivity gains can boost profits even with slow job growth
failure_condition: If this combination of industrial growth and weak employment consistently leads to negative 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction effect is not statistically significant in predicting positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently high federal funds rate (above 4%) indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Higher interest rates generally depress equity valuations
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS consistently above 4% is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the negative correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.
